Juett Tech Rescue Boulder, CO
How it works Pricing Services Impact About Schedule pickup

Recycle your old electronics, responsibly.

We come to your door, pick up your old tech, and make sure it's recycled — not landfilled. Free pickup on phones, laptops & more.

Why recycling electronics matters

All electronics we rescue helps keep toxic materials out of landfills and recovers rare materials that would otherwise be wasted.

3Total E-Waste Pickups
11.2 lbsE-waste diverted from landfill
765gRaw earth minerals recovered
100%Certified e-waste recycling

10% of profits from every pickup are donated to Basel Action Network (BAN), a nonprofit fighting illegal e-waste dumping and advocating for responsible electronics recycling worldwide.

The process

How It Works & What You Get

No drop-offs. No hassle. We handle everything from your doorstep to responsible recycling.

1

Schedule Online

Fill out our simple form — tell us what you have and when works for you.

2

We Come to You

We show up at your Boulder address on your preferred day and collect your electronics — door to door.

3

Secure Wipe & Recycle

For high-value electronics, your data is professionally wiped and your device responsibly resold. General electronics are responsibly recycled through certified e-waste systems.

4

Get Paid / Give Back

High-value devices with resale value earn YOU a payment instead of a pickup fee. A portion (10%) of profits from every pickup, both high-value and general electronics, is donated to Basel Action Network (BAN).

Proudly Supporting

Basel Action Network (BAN)

Fighting illegal e-waste dumping and advocating for responsible electronics recycling worldwide.

Not sure which category your electronics fall under (Either High-Value or General), or have questions about pricing? Check out our Pricing section below! Electronic types are listed and categorized for easy reference.
Transparent pricing

Simple, Fair Pricing

No surprises. Free pickup for devices with resale value — and you might even earn money.

High-Value Electronics

$0 pickup fee

These devices often have resale value — pickup is always free, and we may pay you depending on condition.

  • Phones & smartphones
  • iPads & tablets
  • Laptops & MacBooks
  • Gaming consoles
  • Apple Watches & smartwatches
  • AirPods & wireless earbuds
  • Graphing calculators
  • Cameras, lenses & drones
  • Newer audio gear & speakers
  • Select newer devices with resale value

General Electronics

$25 pickup fee

A single flat fee covers pickup of all your old general electronics in one visit.

  • Desktop computers & towers
  • Monitors & computer accessories
  • Keyboards, mice & webcams
  • Printers, scanners & peripherals
  • Routers, modems & networking gear
  • Cables, cords, adapters & chargers
  • Power strips, surge protectors & docks
  • Speakers, DVD players & audio gear
  • TV boxes, remotes & streaming devices
  • Old phones, landlines & answering machines
  • Alarm clocks, radios & small gadgets
  • Vacuum-style robot accessories & misc. tech
  • Handheld electronics & battery-powered devices
  • Small appliances such as toasters, blenders, coffee makers & fans
  • And most other common electronic e-waste

Refer a friend, Save $5

Know someone with old electronics? Refer them to Juett Tech Rescue and get $5 off your next pickup — up to 3 referrals, saving you up to $15 total.

Get in touch

Ready to clear out your old tech?

Fill out the form ANYTIME and we'll be in touch shortly to confirm your pickup. Or reach out directly — we're happy to answer any questions.

About the founder

Hi, I'm Natalie

Natalie Juett20 years old · Born in Dallas TX

I am a current sophomore at CU Boulder studying economics and political science. During my first semester of sophomore year, I took an Environmental Sustainability and Features course, where I learned about the serious effects of e-waste on our environment when electronics are disposed of improperly.

The facts are striking: the world generates over 50 million metric tons of electronic waste every year, making it the fastest-growing solid waste stream on the planet. When electronics end up in landfills, toxic materials such as lead, mercury, and cadmium leach into the soil and groundwater, posing serious risks to both human health and local ecosystems. At the same time, rare and valuable metals like gold, silver, and cobalt are permanently lost rather than being recovered and reused.

I love our planet, and the goal of this business is to ensure its preservation and environmental safety through the proper disposal of e-waste. At the same time, I am committed to giving back — 10% of profits from every pickup are donated to Basel Action Network (BAN), a nonprofit dedicated to fighting illegal e-waste dumping and advocating for responsible recycling worldwide. While helping the Earth, I also hope to do my part in making the world a better place through this partnership.

Further reading — e-waste & environmental impact

Global E-waste Monitor 2024 (ITU & UNITAR) — 62 million tonnes of e-waste generated in 2022; less than 23% formally recycled World Health Organization — "Electronic Waste (E-Waste)" Fact Sheet — health impacts of improper e-waste disposal U.S. Environmental Protection Agency — Sustainable Materials Management Electronics Challenge — recovery of rare materials and certified recycling Global E-waste Statistics Partnership (GESP) — international e-waste data, classifications, and reporting indicators
